643            if not _local(mesh0):
644                return prev
645            g_eh_m = jax.tree_util.tree_map(jnp.add, g_embed_m, g_head_on0)
646            return g_eh_m if prev is None else jax.tree_util.tree_map(jnp.add, prev, g_eh_m)
647 
648        # Host-staged fallback (``ppermute`` off): run each cross-host broadcast on a side
649        # thread so the main thread keeps dispatching other microbatches while the hop is in
650        # flight; the consuming op resolves the future just-in-time. Both hosts submit in
651        # identical ``op_order`` so the broadcasts stay paired. ``worker`` is None when the flag
652        # is off, single-host, or ppermute is on, so ``_send`` collapses to the inline path.
653        worker = _TransportWorker() if (transport == TransportMode.ASYNC and _multihost()) else None
654 
655        def _send(x: jax.Array, dst: Mesh, src: Mesh, src_stage: int, dst_stage: int) -> jax.Array | Future:
656            if not _crosses_host(src, dst):
657                return _transport(x, dst)
658            if _NULL_XPORT:
659                spec = grug_model._batch_spec()
660                if _local(dst):
661                    return jax.device_put(jnp.zeros(x.shape, x.dtype), NamedSharding(dst, spec))
662                return _make_global(None, dst, spec, x.shape, x.dtype)
663            hop = ppermute_hops.get(min(src_stage, dst_stage))
664            if hop is not None:
665                # On-device ppermute: a jax.Array that XLA overlaps with compute -- no thread.
666                # from_pp=0 forward (source is the low stage), 1 backward (source is the high).
667                return hop(x, 0 if src_stage < dst_stage else 1)
668            if worker is not None:
669                return worker.submit(x, dst, grug_model._batch_spec())
670            return _transport(x, dst)
671 
672        if use_schedule:
673            # Wavefront: one interleaved dispatch following ``op_order``. F/B/W ops stream
674            # across the stages; reductions are deferred so the single Python dispatch
675            # thread never stalls (an in-loop tree_map serializes the GPUs). With
676            # ``split_w`` the B op is input-grad only and W ops carry the weight-grad;
677            # otherwise B is the combined vjp and writes the weight-grad itself.
678            try:
679                for kind, m, s in op_order:
680                    if kind == _F:
681                        if s == 0 and _local(mesh0):
682                            with set_mesh(mesh0):
683                                saved_x[m][0] = embed_fwd(eh0, tok_mb[m])
684                        if _local(submeshes[s]):
685                            with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
686                                h_out, z_mb[m][s] = stage_fns[s].forward(stage_params[s], _resolve(saved_x[m][s]))
687                        else:
688                            h_out, z_mb[m][s] = _act_ph(submeshes[s]), _scalar_ph(submeshes[s])
689                        if s < num_stages - 1:
690                            saved_x[m][s + 1] = _send(h_out, submeshes[s + 1], submeshes[s], s, s + 1)
691                        else:
692                            head_h[m] = h_out
693                            if _local(meshL):
694                                with set_mesh(meshL):
695                                    ce_mb[m] = head_fwd(ehL, h_out, labels_mb[m], weight_mb[m])
696                            else:
697                                ce_mb[m] = _scalar_ph(meshL)
698                    elif kind == _B:
699                        if s == num_stages - 1:
700                            if _local(meshL):
701                                with set_mesh(meshL):
702                                    g_head_mb[m], dy = head_bwd(ehL, head_h[m], labels_mb[m], weight_mb[m], inv_m)
703                                saved_dy[m][s] = dy
704                            else:
705                                g_head_mb[m], saved_dy[m][s] = ehL, _act_ph(meshL)
706                        if _local(submeshes[s]):
707                            x_s, dy_s = _resolve(saved_x[m][s]), _resolve(saved_dy[m][s])
708                            with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
709                                if split_w:
710                                    dx = stage_fns[s].b(stage_params[s], x_s, dy_s, dz)
711                                else:
712                                    w_grads[m][s], dx = stage_fns[s].backward(stage_params[s], x_s, dy_s, dz)
713                        else:
714                            dx = _act_ph(submeshes[s])
715                        if s > 0:
716                            saved_dy[m][s - 1] = _send(dx, submeshes[s - 1], submeshes[s], s, s - 1)
717                        elif _local(mesh0):
718                            with set_mesh(mesh0):
719                                (g_embed_mb[m],) = embed_bwd(eh0, tok_mb[m], dx)
720                    else:  # _W (emitted only when split_w)
721                        if _local(submeshes[s]):
722                            x_s, dy_s = _resolve(saved_x[m][s]), _resolve(saved_dy[m][s])
723                            with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
724                                w_grads[m][s] = stage_fns[s].w(stage_params[s], x_s, dy_s, dz)
725            finally:
726                if worker is not None:
727                    worker.close()
728 
729            if _TRACE:
730                jax.block_until_ready((ce_mb, z_mb, g_head_mb, g_embed_mb, w_grads))
731                _t["sched"] = time.perf_counter()
732 
733            # Sum the embed/head weight-grads across microbatches LOCALLY, then cross the host
734            # boundary once. The head grad is the [hidden, vocab] unembedding (~hundreds of MB);
735            # accumulating per microbatch would broadcast it num_microbatches times.
736            g_eh = _accum_embed_head(_tree_sum(g_embed_mb), _tree_sum(g_head_mb), None)
737            for s in range(num_stages):
738                if not _local(submeshes[s]):
739                    continue
740                base = s * lps
741                acc = list(w_grads[0][s])
742                for m in range(1, num_microbatches):
743                    acc = [jax.tree_util.tree_map(jnp.add, a, b) for a, b in zip(acc, w_grads[m][s], strict=True)]
744                for j, g in enumerate(acc):
745                    g_blocks[base + j] = g
746        else:
747            # GPipe baseline: full forward sweep, then a combined-vjp backward (B and W
748            # fused, so dx waits on the weight-grad and nothing fills the bubble).
749            for m in range(num_microbatches):
750                with set_mesh(mesh0):
751                    h = embed_fwd(eh0, tok_mb[m])
752                for s in range(num_stages):
753                    if s > 0:
754                        h = _transport(h, submeshes[s])
755                    saved_x[m][s] = h
756                    with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
757                        h, z_mb[m][s] = stage_fns[s].forward(stage_params[s], h)
758                head_h[m] = h
759                with set_mesh(meshL):
760                    ce_mb[m] = head_fwd(ehL, h, labels_mb[m], weight_mb[m])
761 
762            if _TRACE:
763                jax.block_until_ready((head_h, saved_x))
764                _t["fwd"] = time.perf_counter()
765 
766            def _accum_blocks(base, g_slice):
767                for j, g in enumerate(g_slice):
768                    cur = g_blocks[base + j]
769                    g_blocks[base + j] = g if cur is None else jax.tree_util.tree_map(jnp.add, cur, g)
770 
771            for m in range(num_microbatches):
772                with set_mesh(meshL):
773                    g_head_m, d_hidden = head_bwd(ehL, head_h[m], labels_mb[m], weight_mb[m], inv_m)
774                for s in reversed(range(num_stages)):
775                    if s < num_stages - 1:
776                        d_hidden = _transport(d_hidden, submeshes[s])
777                    with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
778                        g_slice, d_hidden = stage_fns[s].backward(stage_params[s], saved_x[m][s], d_hidden, dz)
779                    _accum_blocks(s * lps, g_slice)
780                d_hidden = _transport(d_hidden, mesh0)
781                with set_mesh(mesh0):
782                    (g_embed_m,) = embed_bwd(eh0, tok_mb[m], d_hidden)
783                g_eh = _accum_embed_head(g_embed_m, g_head_m, g_eh)
784 
785        # Muon optimizer: orthogonalize each block's weight-grad on its own stage's
786        # devices. Dispatched block-major (round-robin across stages) so all P stages
787        # run Newton-Schulz concurrently -- no all-gather, unlike FSDP where each weight's
788        # grad must be gathered across the data axis first.
789        if muon_fn is not None:
790            for j in range(lps):
791                for s in range(num_stages):
792                    if not _local(submeshes[s]):
793                        continue
794                    layer = s * lps + j
795                    with set_mesh(submeshes[s]):
796                        g_blocks[layer] = muon_fn(g_blocks[layer])
797 
798        # Loss (deferred reduction; the backward seeds are constants, so this never gates it).
799        # Sum each stage's router z-loss across microbatches LOCALLY, then cross to the head mesh
800        # once per stage (was a broadcast per microbatch x stage -- M*P crossings for scalars).
801        # multi-host then broadcasts the final scalar so every process can read it.
802        z_stage_on_head = [
803            _transport(functools.reduce(jnp.add, (z_mb[m][s] for m in range(num_microbatches))), meshL, _REPLICATED)
804            for s in range(num_stages)
805        ]
806        if _local(meshL):
807            ce_total = functools.reduce(jnp.add, ce_mb)
808            z_total = jnp.sum(jnp.stack(z_stage_on_head))
809            loss = (ce_total + coef * z_total / num_layers) / num_microbatches
810        else:
811            loss = None
812        if _multihost():
813            src = np.asarray(jax.device_get(loss) if _local(meshL) else 0.0, np.float32)
814            loss = float(multihost_utils.broadcast_one_to_all(src, is_source=_local(meshL)))
815 
816        if _TRACE:
817            jax.block_until_ready((loss, g_eh, g_blocks))
818            now = time.perf_counter()
819            total = (now - _t["start"]) * 1e3
820            if use_schedule:
821                sched = (_t["sched"] - _t["start"]) * 1e3
822                print(
823                    f"TRACE {schedule.value} wavefront dispatch+exec={sched:.0f}ms reduce={total - sched:.0f}ms "
824                    f"total={total:.0f}ms (M={num_microbatches} P={num_stages} ops={len(op_order)})"
825                )
826            else:
827                fwd, bwd = (_t["fwd"] - _t["start"]) * 1e3, (now - _t["fwd"]) * 1e3
828                print(
829                    f"TRACE gpipe fwd_sweep={fwd:.0f}ms bwd_sweep={bwd:.0f}ms total={total:.0f}ms "
830                    f"(M={num_microbatches} P={num_stages})"
831                )
832 
833        return loss, g_eh, g_blocks
834 
835    return step
836 
837 
838def zb_value_and_grad(
839    transformer: Transformer,
840    token_ids: jax.Array,
841    loss_weight: jax.Array,
842    *,
843    num_stages: int,
844    num_microbatches: int,
845    expert_per_stage: int = 1,
846    data_per_stage: int = 1,
847    schedule: Schedule = Schedule.ZERO_BUBBLE,
848    remat: bool = True,
849    muon: bool = False,
850) -> tuple[jax.Array | float, tuple | None, list]:
851    """One-shot ``(loss, embed_head_grads, block_grads)`` (builds + steps once).
852 
853    Convenience for tests; a training/perf loop should call :func:`zb_build` once and
854    reuse the returned ``step`` across iterations.
855    """
856    step = zb_build(
857        transformer,
858        num_stages=num_stages,
859        num_microbatches=num_microbatches,
860        expert_per_stage=expert_per_stage,
861        data_per_stage=data_per_stage,
862        schedule=schedule,
863        remat=remat,
864        muon=muon,
865    )
866    return step(token_ids, loss_weight)

Back to top

ppermute_probe.py

added

experiments/grug/modular_opt/ppermute_probe.py
experiments/grug/moe_pp/ppermute_probe.py
tt1# Copyright The Marin Authors
2# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3 
4"""Does cross-host GPU-to-GPU ``ppermute`` work in multi-controller JAX, and how fast?
5 
6The device-group pipeline currently crosses the host boundary by staging the activation
7through host memory (``device_get`` + ``broadcast_one_to_all`` or a TCP send). The right
8move is an on-device NCCL send/recv -- ``jax.lax.ppermute`` -- which uses GPUDirect RDMA
9over InfiniBand and never touches host memory, and which XLA overlaps with compute for
10free. The open question is whether a ``shard_map``+``ppermute`` over a mesh that spans
11both processes actually lowers and runs under multi-controller JAX (each process only
12addresses its local devices), and whether it is meaningfully faster than the host hop.
13 
14This probe builds a ``[pp, *act]`` array on a ``(pp=process_count, data=local)`` mesh --
15pp rank == process -- from on-device shards (no host staging), ``ppermute``s it one hop
16along ``pp`` so each process receives its neighbour's slice, verifies the received tag,
17and times the ``ppermute`` against ``broadcast_one_to_all`` of the same bytes.
18 
19    KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/coreweave-iris-gpu uv run iris --cluster=cw-us-east-02a job run \\
20      --gpu H100x8 --enable-extra-resources --extra gpu --replicas 2 \\
21      -- python -m experiments.grug.moe_pp.ppermute_probe
22"""
23 
24from __future__ import annotations
25 
26import functools
27import logging
28import time
29 
30import jax
31import numpy as np
32from jax.experimental import multihost_utils
33from jax.experimental.shard_map import shard_map
34from jax.sharding import Mesh, NamedSharding
35from jax.sharding import PartitionSpec as P
36 
37from experiments.grug.moe_pp.benchmark import init_distributed
38 
39logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40 
41ACT = (8, 1024, 1536)  # one boundary activation, ~48 MiB f32
42 
43 
44def _build_pp_array(mesh: Mesh, per_shape: tuple[int, ...], fill: float) -> jax.Array:
45    """Assemble ``[pp, *per_shape]`` on ``mesh`` (spec ``P('pp','data')``) from on-device
46    shards -- each process fills only its own ``pp`` slice, so nothing crosses the wire."""
47    pc = mesh.devices.shape[0]
48    shape = (pc, *per_shape)
49    sharding = NamedSharding(mesh, P("pp", "data"))
50    shard_shape = sharding.shard_shape(shape)
51    shards = [
52        jax.device_put(np.full(shard_shape, fill, np.float32), d)
53        for d in mesh.devices.flat
54        if d.process_index == jax.process_index()
55    ]
56    return jax.make_array_from_single_device_arrays(shape, sharding, shards)
57 
58 
59def main() -> int:
60    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
61    init_distributed()
62    pc, pid, ld = jax.process_count(), jax.process_index(), jax.local_device_count()
63    logger.info("ppermute_probe: %d process(es), %d local dev, platform=%s", pc, ld, jax.devices()[0].platform)
64    if pc < 2:
65        logger.info("single process -- nothing cross-host to probe; exiting")
66        return 0
67 
68    mesh = Mesh(np.array(jax.devices()).reshape(pc, ld), ("pp", "data"))
69 
70    # Each process tags its slice with its own pid; after one ppermute hop each process
71    # should hold its neighbour's tag.
72    x = _build_pp_array(mesh, ACT, float(pid))
73    perm = [(i, (i + 1) % pc) for i in range(pc)]
74 
75    @functools.partial(shard_map, mesh=mesh, in_specs=P("pp", "data"), out_specs=P("pp", "data"))
76    def hop(a):
77        return jax.lax.ppermute(a, "pp", perm)
78 
79    hop = jax.jit(hop)
80    y = jax.block_until_ready(hop(x))
81 
82    # Verify: this process's pp slice now carries (pid-1) mod pc.
83    expected = float((pid - 1) % pc)
84    got = float(np.asarray(jax.device_get(y[pid])).flat[0])
85    ok = abs(got - expected) < 1e-6
86    logger.info(
87        "ppermute correctness: process %d received tag %.0f (expected %.0f) -> %s",
88        pid,
89        got,
90        expected,
91        "OK" if ok else "WRONG",
92    )
93 
94    iters = 50
95 
96    def time_ppermute():
97        t0 = time.perf_counter()
98        for _ in range(iters):
99            out = hop(x)
100        jax.block_until_ready(out)
101        return (time.perf_counter() - t0) / iters
102 
103    def time_broadcast():
104        host = np.full(ACT, float(pid), np.float32)
105        t0 = time.perf_counter()
106        for _ in range(iters):
107            _ = multihost_utils.broadcast_one_to_all(host, is_source=(pid == 0))
108        return (time.perf_counter() - t0) / iters
109 
110    for _ in range(3):
111        hop(x)
112    jax.block_until_ready(hop(x))
113    pp_sec = time_ppermute()
114    bc_sec = time_broadcast()
115    mb = np.prod(ACT) * 4 / 2**20
116    logger.info(
117        "PERF process %d: ppermute=%.3f ms | broadcast_one_to_all=%.3f ms | %.0f MiB | speedup=%.1fx | correct=%s",
118        pid,
119        pp_sec * 1e3,
120        bc_sec * 1e3,
121        mb,
122        bc_sec / pp_sec if pp_sec else float("nan"),
123        ok,
124    )
125 
126    # The real boundary touches only 4 of the 16 GPUs: stage 3's two (last of host 0) and
127    # stage 4's two (first of host 1). Does a shard_map over that 4-device SUBSET lower and
128    # run under multi-controller? If so the pipeline can ppermute the exact 2->2 hop without
129    # dragging the other 12 GPUs into the collective.
130    if pc == 2 and ld >= 2:
131        devs = jax.devices()
132        boundary = np.array([devs[ld - 2], devs[ld - 1], devs[ld], devs[ld + 1]]).reshape(2, 2)
133        bmesh = Mesh(boundary, ("pp", "data"))
134        bx = _build_pp_array(bmesh, ACT, float(pid))
135 
136        @functools.partial(shard_map, mesh=bmesh, in_specs=P("pp", "data"), out_specs=P("pp", "data"))
137        def bhop(a):
138            return jax.lax.ppermute(a, "pp", [(0, 1), (1, 0)])
139 
140        bhop = jax.jit(bhop)
141        try:
142            by = jax.block_until_ready(bhop(bx))
143            bgot = float(np.asarray(jax.device_get(by[pid])).flat[0])
144            bok = abs(bgot - float((pid - 1) % 2)) < 1e-6
145            for _ in range(3):
146                bhop(bx)
147            jax.block_until_ready(bhop(bx))
148            t0 = time.perf_counter()
149            for _ in range(iters):
150                bout = bhop(bx)
151            jax.block_until_ready(bout)
152            bsec = (time.perf_counter() - t0) / iters
153            logger.info(
154                "PERF process %d SUBSET(4-dev boundary): ppermute=%.3f ms | correct=%s",
155                pid,
156                bsec * 1e3,
157                bok,
158            )
159        except Exception as exc:  # the multi-controller subset question -- record the failure mode
160            logger.info("SUBSET(4-dev boundary) process %d FAILED to lower/run: %r", pid, exc)
161    return 0
162 
163 
164if __name__ == "__main__":
165    raise SystemExit(main())

Back to top

thread_probe.py

added

experiments/grug/modular_opt/thread_probe.py
experiments/grug/moe_pp/thread_probe.py
tt1# Copyright The Marin Authors
2# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3 
4"""De-risk threaded host dispatch: can a background thread move arrays (device_get /
5device_put) while the main thread keeps dispatching jit compute on the same devices?
6 
7The device-group multi-host pipeline serializes at the host boundary because the
8transport (``device_get`` + cross-host broadcast) runs on the single dispatch thread
9and blocks it. The proposed fix is a per-host transport thread that runs the boundary
10hop off the critical path while the main thread keeps the local GPUs busy with other
11microbatches -- safe to interleave because every host follows the same 1f1b schedule,
12so the cross-host collectives stay ordered.
13 
14This probe tests only the *mechanics* on one host (no cross-host collective): a worker
15thread pulls a still-computing array to the host and pushes it back, while the main
16thread dispatches a long chain of independent jit matmuls. It asserts (1) no deadlock /
17error from concurrent dispatch + transfer, and (2) the worker's transported result is
18numerically correct. It does NOT prove GPU NCCL-concurrency safety -- that needs the
192-node run -- but a failure here kills the approach before spending a cycle.
20 
21    XLA_FLAGS=--xla_force_host_platform_device_count=8 JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu \\
22      uv run python -m experiments.grug.moe_pp.thread_probe
23"""
24 
25from __future__ import annotations
26 
27import logging
28import queue
29import threading
30import time
31 
32import jax
33import jax.numpy as jnp
34import numpy as np
35 
36logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37 
38N = 2048
39CHAIN = 200
40ITEMS = 16
41 
42 
43def main() -> int:
44    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
45    devs = jax.local_devices()
46    if len(devs) < 2:
47        raise RuntimeError(f"need >=2 local devices, got {len(devs)}")
48    dst = devs[1]
49    logger.info("thread_probe on %d %s device(s)", len(devs), devs[0].platform)
50 
51    @jax.jit
52    def heavy(x):
53        # A dependent chain so the array is genuinely still computing when the worker
54        # asks for it -- forces device_get to wait, the exact blocking we must hide.
55        for _ in range(CHAIN):
56            x = jnp.tanh(x @ x.T) / N
57        return x
58 
59    inbox: queue.Queue = queue.Queue()
60    outbox: queue.Queue = queue.Queue()
61    errors: list = []
62 
63    def worker():
64        # Mirrors the transport thread: pull a (possibly still-computing) on-device array
65        # to the host, then place it onto another device -- the device_get/device_put pair
66        # that a real boundary hop runs (minus the cross-host broadcast).
67        try:
68            while True:
69                item = inbox.get()
70                if item is None:
71                    return
72                idx, arr = item
73                host = np.asarray(jax.device_get(arr))
74                back = jax.device_put(host, dst)
75                outbox.put((idx, back, host))
76        except Exception as exc:  # surface to the main thread, then re-raise
77            errors.append(exc)
78            outbox.put(None)
79 
80    t = threading.Thread(target=worker, daemon=True)
81    t.start()
82 
83    rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
84    inputs = [jnp.asarray(rng.standard_normal((N, N), np.float32)) for _ in range(ITEMS)]
85 
86    start = time.perf_counter()
87    # Main thread: dispatch every heavy() (async) and hand each result to the worker,
88    # never blocking on a transfer -- then drain the worker's results.
89    dispatched = [heavy(x) for x in inputs]
90    for idx, arr in enumerate(dispatched):
91        inbox.put((idx, arr))
92    inbox.put(None)
93 
94    results: dict = {}
95    for _ in range(ITEMS):
96        got = outbox.get()
97        if got is None:
98            break
99        idx, back, host = got
100        results[idx] = (back, host)
101    t.join(timeout=30)
102    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
103 
104    if errors:
105        raise errors[0]
106    if t.is_alive():
107        raise RuntimeError("worker thread did not finish -- deadlock between dispatch and transfer")
108    if len(results) != ITEMS:
109        raise RuntimeError(f"transported {len(results)}/{ITEMS} items")
110 
111    # The transported array (host->dst) must equal a direct device_get of the source.
112    max_err = 0.0
113    for idx in range(ITEMS):
114        back, host = results[idx]
115        ref = np.asarray(jax.device_get(dispatched[idx]))
116        max_err = max(max_err, float(np.max(np.abs(np.asarray(back) - ref))))
117        assert back.devices() == {dst}, f"item {idx} landed on {back.devices()}, not {dst}"
118    logger.info(
119        "PASS: %d items transported concurrently with %d dispatched chains in %.2fs | max_err=%.2e",
120        ITEMS,
121        ITEMS,
122        elapsed,
123        max_err,
124    )
125    if max_err > 1e-5:
126        raise RuntimeError(f"transported values diverged: max_err={max_err:.2e}")
127    return 0
128 
129 
130if __name__ == "__main__":
131    raise SystemExit(main())

launch.py

removed

experiments/grug/modular_opt/launch.py
experiments/grug/moe_pp/launch.py
t1# Copyright The Marin Authorst
2# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3 
4"""Template: grug-modular-opt trial run.
5 
6This variant shows how to use optax.multi_transform to configure multiple
7optimizers for different modules. See #3075 for more context.
8"""
9 
10import dataclasses
11import os
12from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13from datetime import timedelta
14 
15import jax
16import jmp
17import optax
18from fray.cluster import ResourceConfig
19from levanter.callbacks.profiler import ProfilerConfig
20from levanter.checkpoint import CheckpointerConfig
21from levanter.data.text import LmDataConfig
22from levanter.optim import OptimizerConfig
23from levanter.tracker import TrackerConfig
24from levanter.tracker.wandb import WandbConfig
25from levanter.trainer import TrainerConfig
26from levanter.utils.jax_utils import leaf_key_paths
27from marin.execution.executor import executor_main
28from marin.execution.types import ExecutorStep, this_output_path, versioned
29from marin.processing.tokenize import add_validation_sets_to_mixture
30from marin.training.training import temporary_checkpoint_base_path
31 
32from experiments.defaults import default_validation_sets
33from experiments.grug.modular_opt.model import GrugModelConfig
34from experiments.grug.modular_opt.train import GrugEvalConfig, GrugRunConfig, GrugTrainerConfig, run_grug
35from experiments.pretraining_datasets import nemotron_mix
36 
37 
38@OptimizerConfig.register_subclass("grug_param_group_adam")
39@dataclass(frozen=True)
40class GrugParamGroupAdamConfig(OptimizerConfig):
41    """AdamW with path-based parameter groups.
42 
43    Group routing is string-pattern based, so one rule can apply to many layers
44    without listing each layer explicitly.
45    """
46 
47    beta1: float = 0.9
48    beta2: float = 0.95
49    epsilon: float = 1e-8
50    max_grad_norm: float | None = 1.0
51 
52    embed_head_lr_multiplier: float = 0.5
53    embed_head_weight_decay_multiplier: float = 0.0
54    embed_head_beta1: float = 0.5
55    embed_head_beta2: float = 0.95
56 
57    special_lr_multiplier: float = 5.0
58    special_weight_decay_multiplier: float = 0.0
59    special_beta1: float = 0.9
60    special_beta2: float = 0.99
61 
62    embed_head_patterns: tuple[str, ...] = ("embed", "lm_head")
63    special_patterns: tuple[str, ...] = ("scalar", "gate", "lambda")
64    no_decay_patterns: tuple[str, ...] = ("norm", "bias")
65 
66    def build(self, num_train_steps):
67        lr_schedule = self.lr_scheduler(num_train_steps)
68        embed_patterns = tuple(pattern.lower() for pattern in self.embed_head_patterns)
69        special_patterns = tuple(pattern.lower() for pattern in self.special_patterns)
70        no_decay_patterns = tuple(pattern.lower() for pattern in self.no_decay_patterns)
71 
72        def _group_transform(
73            *,
74            learning_rate: float,
75            weight_decay_multiplier: float,
76            beta1: float,
77            beta2: float,
78            lr_multiplier: float,
79        ) -> optax.GradientTransformation:
80            components: list[optax.GradientTransformation] = [
81                optax.scale_by_adam(b1=beta1, b2=beta2, eps=self.epsilon),
82            ]
83            decayed_weight = self.weight_decay * weight_decay_multiplier
84            if decayed_weight > 0:
85                components.append(optax.add_decayed_weights(decayed_weight))
86            components.append(optax.scale(-learning_rate * lr_multiplier))
87            return optax.chain(*components)
88 
89        def _create_mask(params):
90            paths = leaf_key_paths(params)
91 
92            def _label_for_path(_, path):
93                path_str = ".".join(path) if isinstance(path, (list, tuple)) else str(path)
94                path_lower = path_str.lower()
95                if any(pattern in path_lower for pattern in no_decay_patterns):
96                    return "no_decay"
97                if any(pattern in path_lower for pattern in special_patterns):
98                    return "special"
99                if any(pattern in path_lower for pattern in embed_patterns):
100                    return "embed_head"
101                return "default"
102 
103            return jax.tree.map(_label_for_path, params, paths)
104 
105        def _optimizer(base_learning_rate):
106            transforms = {
107                "default": _group_transform(
108                    learning_rate=base_learning_rate,
109                    weight_decay_multiplier=1.0,
110                    beta1=self.beta1,
111                    beta2=self.beta2,
112                    lr_multiplier=1.0,
113                ),
114                "no_decay": _group_transform(
115                    learning_rate=base_learning_rate,
116                    weight_decay_multiplier=0.0,
117                    beta1=self.beta1,
118                    beta2=self.beta2,
119                    lr_multiplier=1.0,
120                ),
121                "embed_head": _group_transform(
122                    learning_rate=base_learning_rate,
123                    weight_decay_multiplier=self.embed_head_weight_decay_multiplier,
124                    beta1=self.embed_head_beta1,
125                    beta2=self.embed_head_beta2,
126                    lr_multiplier=self.embed_head_lr_multiplier,
127                ),
128                "special": _group_transform(
129                    learning_rate=base_learning_rate,
130                    weight_decay_multiplier=self.special_weight_decay_multiplier,
131                    beta1=self.special_beta1,
132                    beta2=self.special_beta2,
133                    lr_multiplier=self.special_lr_multiplier,
134                ),
135            }
136            grouped = optax.multi_transform(transforms, _create_mask)
137            if self.max_grad_norm is None:
138                return grouped
139            return optax.chain(optax.clip_by_global_norm(self.max_grad_norm), grouped)
140 
141        return optax.inject_hyperparams(_optimizer)(base_learning_rate=lr_schedule)
142 
143 
144@dataclass(frozen=True)
145class GrugModularOptLaunchConfig:
146    """Last-mile run config for the modular-opt grug variant."""
147 
148    model: GrugModelConfig
149    data: LmDataConfig
150    output_path: str
151    run_id: str
152    resources: ResourceConfig
153    steps: int
154    batch_size: int
155    seed: int
156    mp: str  # jmp policy string, e.g. "params=float32,compute=bfloat16,output=bfloat16".
157    tracker: TrackerConfig
158    optimizer: OptimizerConfig
159    grug_trainer: GrugTrainerConfig = field(default_factory=GrugTrainerConfig)
160    eval: GrugEvalConfig | None = field(default_factory=GrugEvalConfig)
161 
162 
163GRUG_130M_MODEL = GrugModelConfig(
164    vocab_size=128_256,
165    hidden_dim=512,
166    intermediate_dim=1792,
167    num_layers=6,
168    num_heads=8,
169    num_kv_heads=8,
170    max_seq_len=4096,
171    head_dim=None,
172)
173 
174NEMOTRON_MIX_WITH_DEFAULT_VALIDATION = add_validation_sets_to_mixture(
175    nemotron_mix,
176    default_validation_sets(tokenizer=nemotron_mix.tokenizer),
177)
178 
179 
180def _resolve_run_id(default_run_id: str) -> str:
181    """Resolve run id and append `FERRY_DATE` when launching from ferry workflows."""
182    run_id = os.environ.get("GRUG_RUN_ID", default_run_id)
183    ferry_date = os.environ.get("FERRY_DATE")
184    if ferry_date:
185        run_id = f"{run_id}-{ferry_date}"
186    return run_id
187 
188 
189def _resolve_tracker(tracker: TrackerConfig, run_id: str) -> TrackerConfig:
190    if isinstance(tracker, WandbConfig):
191        return dataclasses.replace(tracker, name=run_id)
192    return tracker
193 
194 
195def run_grug_modular_opt_trial(config: GrugModularOptLaunchConfig) -> None:
196    # Map template launch knobs onto full Levanter TrainerConfig.
197    trainer = TrainerConfig(
198        id=config.run_id,
199        seed=config.seed,
200        train_batch_size=config.batch_size,
201        num_train_steps=config.steps,
202        profiler=ProfilerConfig(enabled=False, start_step=5, num_steps=100, perfetto_link=False),
203        mp=jmp.get_policy(config.mp),
204        tracker=_resolve_tracker(config.tracker, config.run_id),
205        use_explicit_mesh_axes=True,
206        require_accelerator=True,
207        allow_nondivisible_batch_size=False,
208        checkpointer=CheckpointerConfig(
209            base_path=os.path.join(config.output_path, "checkpoints"),
210            temporary_base_path=temporary_checkpoint_base_path(config.output_path),
211            append_run_id_to_base_path=False,
212            save_interval=timedelta(minutes=10),
213            keep=None,
214        ),
215    )
216 
217    grug_trainer = dataclasses.replace(config.grug_trainer, trainer=trainer)
218 
219    run_config = GrugRunConfig(
220        model=config.model,
221        data=config.data,
222        resources=config.resources,
223        optimizer=config.optimizer,
224        trainer=grug_trainer,
225        eval=config.eval,
226    )
227    run_grug(run_config)
228 
229 
230RESOLVED_RUN_ID = _resolve_run_id("grug-modular-opt-trial")
231 
232 
233grug_modular_opt_trial = ExecutorStep(
234    name="grug/modular-opt-trial",
235    fn=run_grug_modular_opt_trial,
236    config=GrugModularOptLaunchConfig(
237        model=versioned(GRUG_130M_MODEL),
238        data=NEMOTRON_MIX_WITH_DEFAULT_VALIDATION,
239        # this_output_path() resolves to this step's output root (e.g. gs://.../grug/modular-opt-trial-<version>).
240        output_path=this_output_path(),
241        # Keep run id out of versioning so changing job metadata doesn't create a new output path.
242        run_id=RESOLVED_RUN_ID,
243        resources=versioned(ResourceConfig.with_tpu("v4-8")),
244        steps=versioned(2_000),
245        batch_size=versioned(512),
246        seed=versioned(0),
247        mp=versioned("params=float32,compute=bfloat16,output=bfloat16"),
248        tracker=WandbConfig(
249            project="marin",
250            tags=["grug", "template", "modular_opt", "issue-3075"],
251            group="grug-modular-opt-trial",
252            name=None,  # filled from run_id in _resolve_tracker
253            replicate_path=this_output_path(),
254        ),
255        optimizer=versioned(
256            GrugParamGroupAdamConfig(
257                learning_rate=3e-3,
258                weight_decay=0.1,
259                lr_schedule="cosine",
260                decay=0.2,
261                min_lr_ratio=0.1,
262                warmup=1000,
263                embed_head_lr_multiplier=0.5,
264                embed_head_weight_decay_multiplier=0.0,
265                embed_head_beta1=0.5,
266                embed_head_beta2=0.95,
267                special_lr_multiplier=5.0,
268                special_weight_decay_multiplier=0.0,
269                special_beta1=0.9,
270                special_beta2=0.99,
271                special_patterns=("scalar", "gate", "lambda"),
272            )
273        ),
274        grug_trainer=versioned(
275            GrugTrainerConfig(
276                z_loss_weight=1e-4,
277                ema_beta=None,
278                log_every=1,
279            )
280        ),
281        eval=versioned(
282            GrugEvalConfig(
283                eval_batch_size=512,
284                steps_per_eval=1000,
285                max_eval_batches=8,
286                eval_current=True,
287                eval_ema=False,
288            )
289        ),
290    ),
291)
292 
293 
294if __name__ == "__main__":
295    executor_main(
296        steps=[grug_modular_opt_trial],
297        description="Template grug modular-opt 130M trial run (~2000 steps) with parameter-group Adam.",
298    )

t1# Copyright The Marin Authorst
2# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3 
4import dataclasses
5from dataclasses import dataclass
6 
7import equinox as eqx
8import jax
9import jax.numpy as jnp
10from einops import rearrange
11from haliax.jax_utils import named_call
12from jax import random
13from jax.sharding import PartitionSpec as P
14from jax.sharding import reshard
15from jaxtyping import Array, Float, Int, PRNGKeyArray
16from levanter.grug.attention import AttentionMask, RotaryConfig, apply_rotary_embedding, attention
17from levanter.grug.loss import fused_linear_softmax_cross_entropy_loss
18from levanter.grug.sharding import Pbatch, Pembed_vocab, Plm_head, Plogits, unshard
19 
20 
21@dataclass(frozen=True)
22class GrugModelConfig:
23    """Hyperparameters for the Grug Llama-style transformer."""
24 
25    vocab_size: int
26    hidden_dim: int = 2048
27    intermediate_dim: int = 5632
28    num_layers: int = 24
29    num_heads: int = 16
30    num_kv_heads: int = 16
31    head_dim: int | None = None
32    max_seq_len: int = 4096
33    layer_norm_eps: float = 1e-5
34    initializer_std: float = 0.02
35    rope: RotaryConfig = dataclasses.field(default_factory=RotaryConfig)
36 
37    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
38        _ = self.inferred_head_dim
39        if self.num_heads % self.num_kv_heads != 0:
40            raise ValueError("num_heads must be divisible by num_kv_heads for grouped-query attention")
41        if self.vocab_size <= 0:
42            raise ValueError("vocab_size must be positive")
43        if self.max_seq_len <= 0:
44            raise ValueError("max_seq_len must be positive")
45 
46    @property
47    def inferred_head_dim(self) -> int:
48        if self.head_dim is not None:
49            return self.head_dim
50        if self.hidden_dim % self.num_heads != 0:
51            raise ValueError(
52                f"hidden_dim={self.hidden_dim} is not divisible by num_heads={self.num_heads}; set head_dim explicitly"
53            )
54        return self.hidden_dim // self.num_heads
55 
56 
57class CausalSelfAttention(eqx.Module):
58    w_q: jax.Array
59    w_k: jax.Array
60    w_v: jax.Array
61    w_o: jax.Array
62    cfg: GrugModelConfig = eqx.field(static=True)
63 
64    @staticmethod
65    def init(cfg: GrugModelConfig, *, key: PRNGKeyArray) -> "CausalSelfAttention":
66        k_q, k_k, k_v, k_o = random.split(key, 4)
67        d_model, n_heads, n_kv_heads, head_dim = cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.num_heads, cfg.num_kv_heads, cfg.inferred_head_dim
68        return CausalSelfAttention(
69            w_q=reshard(_init_weight(k_q, (d_model, n_heads * head_dim), cfg.initializer_std), P("data", "model")),
70            w_k=reshard(_init_weight(k_k, (d_model, n_kv_heads * head_dim), cfg.initializer_std), P("data", "model")),
71            w_v=reshard(_init_weight(k_v, (d_model, n_kv_heads * head_dim), cfg.initializer_std), P("data", "model")),
72            w_o=reshard(_init_weight(k_o, (n_heads * head_dim, d_model), cfg.initializer_std), P("model", "data")),
73            cfg=cfg,
74        )
75 
76    @named_call
77    def __call__(self, x: Float[Array, "B S D"], mask: AttentionMask | jax.Array) -> Float[Array, "B S D"]:
78        head_dim = self.cfg.inferred_head_dim
79        seq_len = x.shape[1]
80 
81        q = rearrange(jnp.einsum("bsh,hd->bsd", x, self.w_q), "... (n d) -> ... n d", d=head_dim)
82        k = rearrange(jnp.einsum("bsh,hd->bsd", x, self.w_k), "... (m d) -> ... m d", d=head_dim)
83        v = rearrange(jnp.einsum("bsh,hd->bsd", x, self.w_v), "... (m d) -> ... m d", d=head_dim)
84        q, k = apply_rotary_embedding(q, k, seq_len=seq_len, head_dim=head_dim, rope=self.cfg.rope)
85        attn_out = attention(q, k, v, mask)
86        attn_out = rearrange(attn_out, "... n d -> ... (n d)")
87        return jnp.einsum("bsh,hd->bsd", attn_out, self.w_o, out_sharding=Pbatch)
88 
89 
90class MLP(eqx.Module):
91    mlp_up: jax.Array
92    mlp_down: jax.Array
93 
94    @staticmethod
95    def init(cfg: GrugModelConfig, *, key: PRNGKeyArray) -> "MLP":
96        k_up, k_down = random.split(key, 2)
97        d_model, d_ff = cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.intermediate_dim
98        return MLP(
99            mlp_up=reshard(_init_weight(k_up, (d_model, d_ff), cfg.initializer_std), P("data", "model")),
100            mlp_down=reshard(_init_weight(k_down, (d_ff, d_model), cfg.initializer_std), P("model", "data")),
101        )
102 
103    @named_call
104    def __call__(self, x: Float[Array, "B S D"]) -> Float[Array, "B S D"]:
105        up = jnp.einsum("bsh,hm->bsm", x, self.mlp_up)
106        activated = jax.nn.relu(up)
107        return jnp.einsum("bsm,mh->bsh", activated, self.mlp_down, out_sharding=Pbatch)
108 
109 
110class RMSNorm(eqx.Module):
111    weight: jax.Array
112    eps: float = eqx.field(static=True)
113 
114    @staticmethod
115    def init(dim: int, eps: float) -> "RMSNorm":
116        return RMSNorm(weight=jnp.ones((dim,), dtype=jnp.float32), eps=eps)
117 
118    @named_call
119    def __call__(self, x: Float[Array, "... D"]) -> Float[Array, "... D"]:
120        weight = unshard(self.weight)
121        dtype = x.dtype
122        x = x.astype(jnp.float32)
123        variance = jnp.mean(jnp.square(x), axis=-1, keepdims=True)
124        normed = x * jax.lax.rsqrt(variance + self.eps)
125        return (normed * weight).astype(dtype)
126 
127 
128class Block(eqx.Module):
129    rms_attn: RMSNorm
130    attn: CausalSelfAttention
131    rms_mlp: RMSNorm
132    mlp: MLP
133 
134    @staticmethod
135    def init(cfg: GrugModelConfig, *, key: PRNGKeyArray) -> "Block":
136        attn_key, mlp_key = random.split(key, 2)
137        return Block(
138            rms_attn=RMSNorm.init(cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.layer_norm_eps),
139            attn=CausalSelfAttention.init(cfg, key=attn_key),
140            rms_mlp=RMSNorm.init(cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.layer_norm_eps),
141            mlp=MLP.init(cfg, key=mlp_key),
142        )
143 
144    @named_call
145    def __call__(self, x: Float[Array, "B S D"], mask: AttentionMask | jax.Array) -> Float[Array, "B S D"]:
146        x = x + self.attn(self.rms_attn(x), mask)
147        x = x + self.mlp(self.rms_mlp(x))
148        return x
149 
150 
151class Transformer(eqx.Module):
152    token_embed: jax.Array
153    output_proj: jax.Array
154    blocks: tuple[Block, ...]
155    final_norm: RMSNorm
156    config: GrugModelConfig = eqx.field(static=True)
157 
158    @staticmethod
159    def init(cfg: GrugModelConfig, *, key: PRNGKeyArray) -> "Transformer":
160        embed_key, out_key, *block_keys = random.split(key, cfg.num_layers + 2)
161        token_embed = reshard(
162            _init_weight(embed_key, (cfg.vocab_size, cfg.hidden_dim), cfg.initializer_std), Pembed_vocab
163        )
164        output_proj = reshard(_init_weight(out_key, (cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.vocab_size), cfg.initializer_std), Plm_head)
165        blocks = tuple(Block.init(cfg, key=layer_key) for layer_key in block_keys)
166        final_norm = RMSNorm.init(cfg.hidden_dim, cfg.layer_norm_eps)
167        return Transformer(
168            token_embed=token_embed,
169            output_proj=output_proj,
170            blocks=blocks,
171            final_norm=final_norm,
172            config=cfg,
173        )
174 
175    @named_call
176    def __call__(
177        self,
178        token_ids: Int[Array, "B S"],
179        mask: AttentionMask | jax.Array | None = None,
180    ) -> Float[Array, "B S D"]:
181        if mask is None:
182            mask = AttentionMask.causal()
183 
184        hidden = self.token_embed.at[token_ids].get(out_sharding=Pbatch)
185        for block in self.blocks:
186            hidden = eqx.filter_checkpoint(block)(hidden, mask)
187        return self.final_norm(hidden)
188 
189    @named_call
190    def logits(
191        self,
192        token_ids: Int[Array, "B S"],
193        mask: AttentionMask | jax.Array | None = None,
194    ) -> Float[Array, "B S V"]:
195        hidden = self(token_ids, mask=mask)
196        return jnp.einsum("bsh,hd->bsd", hidden, self.output_proj, out_sharding=Plogits)
197 
198    def next_token_loss(
199        self,
200        token_ids: Int[Array, "B S"],
201        loss_weight: Float[Array, "B S"],
202        *,
203        mask: AttentionMask | jax.Array | None = None,
204        reduction: str = "mean",
205        logsumexp_weight: float | None = None,
206        loss_dtype: jnp.dtype = jnp.float32,
207    ) -> jax.Array:
208        """Compute next-token cross-entropy loss for a batch."""
209        hidden = self(token_ids, mask=mask)
210        labels = jnp.concatenate([token_ids[:, 1:], token_ids[:, :1] * 0], axis=1).astype(jnp.int32)
211        loss_weight = loss_weight.astype(loss_dtype)
212 
213        return fused_linear_softmax_cross_entropy_loss(
214            hidden,
215            self.output_proj,
216            labels,
217            weight=loss_weight,
218            reduction=reduction,
219            logsumexp_weight=logsumexp_weight,
220            dtype=loss_dtype,
221        )
222 
223 
224def _init_weight(key: PRNGKeyArray, shape: tuple[int, ...], std: float) -> Float[Array, "..."]:
225    return std * random.truncated_normal(key, -3, 3, shape)
226 
227 
228def debug_mesh_and_token_pspec(num_devices: int, model_axis_size: int = 1) -> tuple[jax.sharding.AbstractMesh, P]:
229    """Return a small abstract mesh and token sharding for lowering contract tests."""
230    if num_devices <= 0:
231        raise ValueError(f"num_devices must be positive, got {num_devices}")
232    if model_axis_size <= 0:
233        raise ValueError(f"model_axis_size must be positive, got {model_axis_size}")
234    if num_devices % model_axis_size != 0:
235        raise ValueError(f"num_devices ({num_devices}) must be divisible by model_axis_size ({model_axis_size})")
236    data_axis_size = num_devices // model_axis_size
237    mesh = jax.sharding.AbstractMesh(
238        axis_sizes=(1, data_axis_size, model_axis_size),
239        axis_names=("replica_dcn", "data", "model"),
240        axis_types=(
241            jax.sharding.AxisType.Explicit,
242            jax.sharding.AxisType.Explicit,
243            jax.sharding.AxisType.Explicit,
244        ),
245    )
246    return mesh, P(("replica_dcn", "data"), None)
247 
248 
249__all__ = [
250    "MLP",
251    "Block",
252    "CausalSelfAttention",
253    "GrugModelConfig",
254    "RMSNorm",
255    "Transformer",
256    "debug_mesh_and_token_pspec",
257]

17import optax

19from haliax import Axis

21from jax.sharding import Mesh, NamedSharding

24from jaxtyping import PRNGKeyArray

46 
47 
48@dataclass(frozen=True)
49class GrugTrainerConfig:
50    """Runtime knobs for grug training."""
51 
52    trainer: TrainerConfig = field(default_factory=lambda: TrainerConfig(use_explicit_mesh_axes=True))
53    train_batch_pspec: P = field(default_factory=lambda: P(("replica_dcn", "data")))
54    data_seed: int | None = None
55    log_every: int = 1
56    ema_beta: float | None = None  # EMA coefficient for eval/checkpoint model; None disables EMA.
57    z_loss_weight: float = 0.0  # Weight on logsumexp (z-loss) stabilization term.
58 
59 
60@dataclass(frozen=True)
61class GrugEvalConfig:
62    """Perplexity eval settings for grug training."""
63 
64    eval_batch_size: int = 512
65    eval_batch_pspec: P = field(default_factory=lambda: P(("replica_dcn", "data")))
66    steps_per_eval: int | None = 1000
67    max_eval_batches: int | None = None
68    prefix: str = "eval"
69    eval_current: bool = True
70    eval_ema: bool = True
71    compute_bpb: bool = True
72 
73 
74@dataclass(frozen=True)
75class GrugRunConfig:
76    """Top-level config for grug training."""
77 
78    model: GrugModelConfig
79    data: LmDataConfig
80    resources: ResourceConfig
81    optimizer: OptimizerConfig = field(default_factory=AdamConfig)
82    trainer: GrugTrainerConfig = field(default_factory=GrugTrainerConfig)
83    eval: GrugEvalConfig | None = field(default_factory=GrugEvalConfig)
84 
85 
86def build_train_dataset(
87    data_config: LmDataConfig,
88    *,
89    max_seq_len: int,
90    batch_schedule: BatchSchedule,
91    key: PRNGKeyArray,
92) -> MixtureDataset[GrugLmExample]:
93    pos = Axis("position", max_seq_len)
94    mix_key, shuffle_key = jax.random.split(key)
95    weights = data_config.train_weights
96    if isinstance(weights, list):
97        weights = rescale_mixture_schedule_for_batch_schedule(weights, batch_schedule)
98 
99    initial_batch_size = batch_schedule.batch_size_at_step(0)
100    datasets = data_config.train_sets(pos, key=shuffle_key, initial_batch_size=initial_batch_size)
101    return MixtureDataset(
102        datasets=datasets,
103        weights=weights,
104        stop_strategy=data_config.stop_strategy,
105        key=mix_key,
106        block_size=data_config.mixture_block_size,
107    )
108 
109 
110def build_train_loader(
111    dataset: AsyncDataset[GrugLmExample],
112    *,
113    batch_schedule: BatchSchedule,
114    mesh: Mesh,
115    batch_pspec: P = P(("replica_dcn", "data")),
116) -> DataLoader[GrugLmExample]:
117    # DataLoader uses this batch axis mapping to shard batches across the distributed mesh.
118    axis_resource = batch_pspec[0]
119    return DataLoader(
120        dataset,
121        batch_schedule.schedule,
122        mesh=mesh,
123        axis_resources={"__BATCH__": axis_resource},
124        batch_axis_name="__BATCH__",
125        allow_nondivisible_batch_size=False,
126    )
127 
128 
129def build_tagged_evaluator(
130    *,
131    data_config: LmDataConfig,
132    max_seq_len: int,
133    mesh: Mesh,
134    eval_cfg: GrugEvalConfig,
135) -> TaggedEvaluator[LmExample | GrugLmExample, Transformer] | None:
136    pos = Axis("position", max_seq_len)
137    tagged_eval_sets = data_config.tagged_eval_sets(pos)
138    if len(tagged_eval_sets) == 0:
139        logger.warning("No evaluation datasets provided.")
140        return None
141 
142    max_examples_per_dataset = None
143    if eval_cfg.max_eval_batches is not None:
144        max_examples_per_dataset = eval_cfg.max_eval_batches * eval_cfg.eval_batch_size
145 
146    tokenizer = data_config.the_tokenizer if eval_cfg.compute_bpb else None
147    batch_axis_resource = eval_cfg.eval_batch_pspec[0]
148    eval_axis_mapping = {"batch": batch_axis_resource}
149    eval_batch = Axis("batch", eval_cfg.eval_batch_size)
150    eval_array_sharding = NamedSharding(mesh, P(batch_axis_resource, None))
151 
152    def eval_loss_fn(model: Transformer, batch: LmExample | GrugLmExample) -> tuple[jax.Array, jax.Array, jax.Array]:
153        if isinstance(batch, LmExample):
154            batch = grug_lm_example_from_named(batch)
155        per_pos_loss = model.next_token_loss(
156            batch.tokens,
157            batch.loss_weight,
158            mask=batch.attn_mask,
159            reduction="none",
160            logsumexp_weight=None,
161        )
162        per_pos_loss = jax.sharding.reshard(per_pos_loss, eval_array_sharding)
163        per_pos_weight = jax.sharding.reshard(batch.loss_weight, eval_array_sharding)
164        per_pos_token_id = jnp.roll(batch.tokens, -1, axis=-1)
165        return per_pos_loss, per_pos_weight, per_pos_token_id
166 
167    return TaggedEvaluator(
168        EvalBatch=eval_batch,
169        tagged_eval_sets=tagged_eval_sets,
170        loss_fn=eval_loss_fn,
171        tokenizer=tokenizer,
172        device_mesh=mesh,
173        axis_mapping=eval_axis_mapping,
174        max_examples_per_dataset=max_examples_per_dataset,
175    )
176 
177 
178def _compute_flops(
179    *,
180    model_config: GrugModelConfig,
181) -> tuple[float, dict[str, float]]:
182    flops_per_token = lm_flops_per_token(
183        hidden_dim=model_config.hidden_dim,
184        intermediate_dim=model_config.intermediate_dim,
185        num_layers=model_config.num_layers,
186        num_kv_heads=model_config.num_kv_heads,
187        num_heads=model_config.num_heads,
188        seq_len=model_config.max_seq_len,
189        vocab_size=model_config.vocab_size,
190        glu=False,
191    )
192    flops_per_example = 3 * flops_per_token * model_config.max_seq_len
193 
194    flops_summary: dict[str, float] = {
195        "throughput/flops_per_token_analytic": flops_per_token,
196        "throughput/flops_per_example_analytic": flops_per_example,
197    }
198 
199    return flops_per_example, flops_summary
200 
201 
202def _make_mixture_stage_callback(train_dataset: MixtureDataset, batch_schedule: BatchSchedule):
203    last_mixture_stage = -1
204 
205    def log_mixture_stage(step_info):
206        nonlocal last_mixture_stage
207        seq_index = batch_schedule.global_data_offset_by_step(step_info.step)
208        block_id = seq_index // train_dataset.block_size
209        stage = train_dataset._get_stage_for_block(block_id)
210        if stage == last_mixture_stage:
211            return
212 
213        weights = train_dataset.weight_stages[stage][1]
214        mixture_log = {f"mixture/weight/{name}": weight for name, weight in weights.items()}
215        mixture_log["mixture/stage"] = stage
216        levanter.tracker.log(mixture_log, step=step_info.step)
217        last_mixture_stage = stage
218 
219    return log_mixture_stage
220 
221 
222@register_dataclass
223@dataclass(frozen=True)
224class GrugTrainState:
225    step: jax.Array
226    params: Transformer
227    opt_state: optax.OptState
228    ema_params: Transformer | None
229 
230 
231def initial_state(
232    model_config: GrugModelConfig,
233    *,
234    optimizer: optax.GradientTransformation,
235    mp: jmp.Policy,
236    key: PRNGKeyArray,
237    ema_beta: float | None,
238) -> GrugTrainState:
239    params = mp.cast_to_param(Transformer.init(model_config, key=key))
240    return GrugTrainState(
241        step=jnp.array(0, dtype=jnp.int32),
242        params=params,
243        opt_state=optimizer.init(params),
244        ema_params=params if ema_beta is not None else None,
245    )
246 
247 
248def _make_train_step(
249    optimizer: optax.GradientTransformation,
250    mp: jmp.Policy,
251    *,
252    z_loss_weight: float,
253    ema_beta: float | None,
254    watch_config: WatchConfig | None = None,
255):
256    one = jnp.array(1, dtype=jnp.int32)
257    z_loss = z_loss_weight if z_loss_weight > 0 else None
258    if watch_config is not None:
259        if isinstance(watch_config.watch_targets, str):
260            watch_targets = tuple(t.strip() for t in watch_config.watch_targets.split(","))
261        else:
262            watch_targets = tuple(watch_config.watch_targets)
263    else:
264        watch_targets = ()
265 
266    @functools.partial(jax.jit, donate_argnums=(0,), static_argnames=("compute_watch",))
267    def train_step(state: GrugTrainState, batch, *, compute_watch: bool = False):
268        def loss_fn(params):
269            compute_params = mp.cast_to_compute(params)
270            return compute_params.next_token_loss(
271                batch.tokens,
272                batch.loss_weight,
273                mask=batch.attn_mask,
274                reduction="mean",
275                logsumexp_weight=z_loss,
276            )
277 
278        loss, grads = jax.value_and_grad(loss_fn)(state.params)
279        updates, opt_state = optimizer.update(grads, state.opt_state, state.params)
280        params = optax.apply_updates(state.params, updates)
281 
282        if ema_beta is None:
283            ema_params = None
284        else:
285            if state.ema_params is None:
286                raise ValueError("ema_params must be initialized when ema_beta is set.")
287            ema_params = jax.tree_util.tree_map(
288                lambda old, new: ema_beta * old + (1.0 - ema_beta) * new,
289                state.ema_params,
290                params,
291            )
292 
293        watch_stats = None
294        if watch_config is not None and compute_watch:
295            watch_stats = compute_watch_stats(
296                watch_targets=watch_targets,
297                include_norms=watch_config.include_norms,
298                include_per_parameter_norms=watch_config.include_per_parameter_norms,
299                include_histogram=watch_config.include_histograms,
300                split_scan_layers=watch_config.split_scan_layers,
301                params=state.params,
302                grads=grads,
303                updates=updates,
304                opt_state=state.opt_state,
305                model_tree_type=type(state.params),
306            )
307 
308        next_state = dataclasses.replace(
309            state,
310            step=state.step + one,
311            params=params,
312            opt_state=opt_state,
313            ema_params=ema_params,
314        )
315 
316        return next_state, {"train/loss": loss}, watch_stats
317 
318    return train_step
319 
320 
321def _run_grug_local(config: GrugRunConfig) -> None:
322    """Run grug training locally inside a Fray worker."""
323    trainer = config.trainer.trainer
324    trainer.initialize()
325    levanter.tracker.log_configuration(config)
326 
327    run_id = trainer.id
328    if run_id is None:
329        raise ValueError("trainer.id was not initialized")
330 
331    optimizer = config.optimizer.build(trainer.num_train_steps)
332    watch_config = trainer.watch
333    train_step = _make_train_step(
334        optimizer,
335        trainer.mp,
336        z_loss_weight=config.trainer.z_loss_weight,
337        ema_beta=config.trainer.ema_beta,
338        watch_config=watch_config if watch_config.is_enabled else None,
339    )
340 
341    data_key, model_key = jax.random.split(jax.random.PRNGKey(trainer.seed), 2)
342    if config.trainer.data_seed is not None:
343        data_key = jax.random.PRNGKey(config.trainer.data_seed)
344 
345    # Grug uses raw PartitionSpecs rather than Trainer's logical axis mapping.
346    # Keep the mesh compact so P(("replica_dcn", "data")) spans slices directly.
347    mesh = compact_grug_mesh()
348    with set_mesh(mesh):
349        batch_schedule = trainer.batch_schedule
350 
351        train_dataset = build_train_dataset(
352            config.data,
353            max_seq_len=config.model.max_seq_len,
354            batch_schedule=batch_schedule,
355            key=data_key,
356        )
357        train_loader = build_train_loader(
358            train_dataset,
359            batch_schedule=batch_schedule,
360            mesh=mesh,
361            batch_pspec=config.trainer.train_batch_pspec,
362        )
363 
364        @jax.jit
365        def _init_state(model_rng):
366            return initial_state(
367                config.model,
368                optimizer=optimizer,
369                mp=trainer.mp,
370                key=model_rng,
371                ema_beta=config.trainer.ema_beta,
372            )
373 
374        state = _init_state(model_key)
375 
376        checkpointer = trainer.checkpointer.create(run_id)
377        state = restore_grug_state_from_checkpoint(
378            state,
379            checkpoint_search_paths=trainer.checkpoint_search_paths(run_id),
380            load_checkpoint_setting=trainer.load_checkpoint,
381            mesh=mesh,
382            allow_partial=trainer.allow_partial_checkpoint,
383        )
384 
385        levanter.tracker.log_summary({"parameter_count": parameter_count(state.params)})
386 
387        flops_per_example, flops_summary = _compute_flops(model_config=config.model)
388        levanter.tracker.log_summary(flops_summary)
389 
390        eval_cfg = config.eval
391        evaluator = None
392        if eval_cfg is not None:
393            evaluator = build_tagged_evaluator(
394                data_config=config.data,
395                max_seq_len=config.model.max_seq_len,
396                mesh=mesh,
397                eval_cfg=eval_cfg,
398            )
399 
400        profiler_cfg = trainer.profiler
401        profiler_num_steps = profiler_cfg.resolve_num_profile_steps(num_train_steps=trainer.num_train_steps)
402        profiler_enabled = profiler_cfg.is_enabled and profiler_num_steps > 0
403 
404        log_every = max(1, config.trainer.log_every)
405        iterator = LoadingTimeTrackerIterator(train_loader.iter_from_step(int(state.step)))
406 
407        state_callbacks = StateCallbackRunner[GrugTrainState](
408            step_getter=lambda s: s.step,
409            model_getter=lambda s: s.params,
410            eval_model_getter=lambda s: s.ema_params if s.ema_params is not None else s.params,
411            opt_state_getter=lambda s: s.opt_state,
412        )
413        state_callbacks.add_hook(
414            callbacks.log_performance_stats(config.model.max_seq_len, batch_schedule, flops_per_example),
415            every=log_every,
416        )
417        state_callbacks.add_hook(callbacks.pbar_logger(total=trainer.num_train_steps), every=log_every)
418        state_callbacks.add_hook(callbacks.log_step_info(trainer.num_train_steps), every=log_every)
419        if profiler_enabled:
420            state_callbacks.add_hook(
421                callbacks.profile(
422                    str(trainer.log_dir / run_id / "profiler"),
423                    profiler_cfg.start_step,
424                    profiler_num_steps,
425                    profiler_cfg.perfetto_link,
426                ),
427                every=1,
428            )
429        state_callbacks.add_hook(_make_mixture_stage_callback(train_dataset, batch_schedule), every=1)
430        if evaluator is not None and eval_cfg is not None:
431            interval = eval_cfg.steps_per_eval
432            eval_ema = eval_cfg.eval_ema and config.trainer.ema_beta is not None
433            if interval is not None and interval > 0 and (eval_cfg.eval_current or eval_ema):
434                state_callbacks.add_hook(
435                    cb_tagged_evaluate(
436                        evaluator,
437                        prefix=eval_cfg.prefix,
438                        eval_current=eval_cfg.eval_current,
439                        eval_ema=eval_ema,
440                    ),
441                    every=interval,
442                )
443 
444        last_loss: float | jax.Array = 0.0
445        last_step_duration = 0.0
446 
447        # Main optimization loop.
448        try:
449            while int(state.step) < trainer.num_train_steps:
450                with jax.profiler.TraceAnnotation("load_batch"):
451                    batch = next(iterator)
452                step_start = time.perf_counter()
453                current_step = int(state.step)
454                # grad_watch runs only on its configured interval.
455                compute_watch = (
456                    watch_config.is_enabled and watch_config.interval > 0 and current_step % watch_config.interval == 0
457                )
458                state, metrics, watch_stats = train_step(state, batch, compute_watch=compute_watch)
459                step = int(state.step) - 1
460 
461                jax.block_until_ready(metrics["train/loss"])
462                duration = time.perf_counter() - step_start
463                hook_start = time.perf_counter()
464                with jax.profiler.TraceAnnotation("callbacks"):
465                    state_callbacks.run(state, loss=metrics["train/loss"], step_duration=duration)
466                    last_loss = metrics["train/loss"]
467                    last_step_duration = duration
468                    levanter.tracker.log({"throughput/hook_time": time.perf_counter() - hook_start}, step=step)
469                    levanter.tracker.log({"throughput/loading_time": iterator.this_load_time}, step=step)
470 
471                    if watch_stats is not None:
472                        levanter.tracker.log(watch_stats, step=step)
473 
474                if checkpointer is not None:
475                    checkpointer.on_step(tree=state, step=int(state.step))
476        except BaseException:
477            logger.exception(
478                "Fatal error in grug training loop; skipping final callbacks/checkpoint to preserve root cause"
479            )
480            raise
481        else:
482            # Mirror classic trainer behavior: force callbacks on the last completed step.
483            state_callbacks.run(state, loss=last_loss, step_duration=last_step_duration, force=True)
484            if checkpointer is not None:
485                checkpointer.on_step(tree=state, step=int(state.step), force=True)
486                checkpointer.wait_until_finished()
487 
488    levanter.tracker.current_tracker().finish()
489 
490 
491def run_grug(config: GrugRunConfig) -> None:
492    """Dispatch grug training through Fray jobs."""
493    trainer = config.trainer.trainer
494    if trainer.id is None:
495        raise ValueError("trainer.id must be set before dispatching grug training.")
496 
497    dispatch_grug_training_run(
498        run_id=trainer.id,
499        config=config,
500        local_entrypoint=_run_grug_local,
501        resources=config.resources,
502    )
503 
504 
505__all__ = [
506    "GrugEvalConfig",
507    "GrugRunConfig",
508    "GrugTrainState",
509    "GrugTrainerConfig",
510    "initial_state",
511    "run_grug",
512]

Back to top
